Off Calendar Jan-25-2022 Continued To Feb-23-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-08-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sherry Collins appeared by zoom for Danielle Jones.
Amante Jones (Beneficiary) appeared in court in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that at the last hearing this matter was continued to allow the parties to talk. The parties spoke of the form of communication that took place and their inability to get to a place where they could move forward. Ms. Collins stated that at the end of administration the estate will be a 50/50 split between Amante and Danielle, and that her Client will need authority from the Court to do anything with the assets. Discussion was held regarding Mr. Jones' ability to qualify for a bond. Mr. Jones stated that if it is definitely a 50/50 split and the Court has to approve it, he is okay with Danielle being appointed. The Court informed the parties that it will benefit everyone if they keep looking forward and not look back, and move this administration along. The Court also explained to Mr. Jones the purpose of having a bond in place and spoke of the protection it provides to the estate and the heirs. The Court will appoint Danielle Jones as the Administrator of the estate with full authority. Bond is at $270,000.00. Status on close of estate is set for January 25, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Letters of Administration is under submission with the Court's intent to grant the petition.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

REQUEST FOR HEARING ABOUT COURT FEE WAIVER ORDER
Off Calendar Nov-20-2020 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-10-2020 Continued To Dec-08-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: sherry Collins appeared by zoom for Danielle Jones.
Amante Jones in Propria Persona (in court).

The Court noted that Mr. Jones filed an objection which indicates that he doesn't know what Ms. Jones' intentions are, that he would like to be involved in the process and that he would like to be a Co-Administrator. Ms. Collins stated that this is the first time that she knows about Mr. Jones' objections, that her Client and Mr. Jones are half siblings, that there is no will and that she believes it will be a 50/50 split in the end. Ms. Collins also stated that the difficulties regarding the bond have been resolved, and that she is happy to talk with Mr. Jones and try and resolve this. Mr. Jones stated that he did not provide Ms. Collins with a copy of his objections. The Court explained to Mr. Jones that he too would need to be bonded and stated that it will continue this matter for the parties to talk. Ms. Collins suggested a 30 to 60-day continuance. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to December 8, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any additional filings are to be filed by December 1, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Jaime. Not reported.
